err.folder.invalid: Error: {path} is not a valid folder or does not exist.
err.file.not_found: Error: Backup file {path} does not exist.
err.dest.invalid: Error: Target folder {path} is not a valid folder or does not exist.
info.already.added: Info: {path} has already been added. Please do not duplicate.
warn.no.strategy.found: Warning: Backup strategy for {path} not found.
warn.source.missing: Warning: Source folder does not exist: {path}
warn.json.decode.error: Warning: Data file {path} is malformed, initializing as empty dict.
warn.json.backup: Warning: Corrupted data file backed up to {path}.
warn.json.renamed: Warning: Corrupted data file renamed to {path}.
warn.invalid.compresslevel: Warning: Compression level {level} out of range (0-9), using default 6
cmd.save.uptodate: All backup strategies are up to date, no backup needed.
restore.list.title: Archive contents: {path}
cmd.watch.start: Watch started, interval {interval} min (press Ctrl+C to stop)
cli.description:
Sbackup v{version} — Lightweight incremental backup tool

Sbackup helps you easily manage multi-folder backup tasks. It uses incremental backup strategy,
only compressing modified files, supporting custom ignore rules, and providing intuitive progress feedback.

Available commands:
  add     Add a new backup strategy (source -> destination)
  rm      Remove an existing backup strategy
  all     Show all configured backup strategies
  save    Execute backup tasks
  version Show version information
cli.epilog:
Examples:
  Add backup strategy:
    sbackup add F:/my_folder F:/backup -i node_modules,.git

  Add strategy with entry-level format:
    sbackup add F:/my_folder F:/backup --format tar.gz

  Remove backup strategy:
    sbackup rm F:/my_folder

  View all strategies:
    sbackup all

  Execute backup (ZIP):
    sbackup save

  Execute backup (tar.gz):
    sbackup --format tar.gz save

  Execute backup (7z encrypted):
    sbackup --format 7z save --password 123456

  Keep last 5 backups:
    sbackup save --keep 5

  Watch backup (every 60 min):
    sbackup watch --interval 60

  Watch + cleanup:
    sbackup watch --interval 120 --keep 10

  Restore backup:
    sbackup restore backup.7z F:/restored

  Chinese interface:
    sbackup --lang zh_CN save

  Debug mode:
    sbackup --debug save

  SFTP remote backup (auto-detect key, recommended):
    sbackup sftp config --host 192.168.1.100 --user admin --remote-path /backups
    sbackup sftp test
    sbackup save --sftp

  SFTP remote backup (password auth):
    sbackup sftp config --host 192.168.1.100 --user admin --password secret
    sbackup save --sftp

  SFTP remote backup (specify key):
    sbackup sftp config --host 192.168.1.100 --user admin --key-file ~/.ssh/id_rsa
    sbackup save --sftp

  SFTP remote backup (key + passphrase):
    sbackup sftp config --host 192.168.1.100 --user admin --key-file ~/.ssh/id_rsa --key-passphrase mypass
    sbackup save --sftp

  SFTP watch and upload:
    sbackup watch --interval 60 --sftp
cli.help.debug: Enable debug mode with detailed logging (can be placed after subcommand)
cli.help.help: Show this help message and exit
cli.help.lang: Set interface language: zh_CN / en_US / fr_FR / es_ES / ru_RU / de_DE / ja_JP / pt_BR / ko_KR
cli.help.format: Set packaging format: zip(default) / tar / tar.gz / tar.bz2 / tar.xz / tar.zst / 7z(encrypted)
cli.help.add.source: Source folder path: the directory to be backed up
cli.help.add.dest: Target folder path: where backup files will be stored
cli.help.add.ignore: Files or folders to ignore (comma separated, default: .git,__pycache__)
cli.help.add.format: Entry-level packaging format (only for this strategy, defaults to global): zip / tar / tar.gz / tar.bz2 / tar.xz / tar.zst / 7z
cli.help.rm.path: Source folder path of the strategy to remove
cli.help.save.keep: Keep the most recent N backup files, 0 to disable cleanup (default: 0)
cli.help.save.password: Encryption password (7z format only, can also be set in config.json)
cli.help.watch.keep: Keep the most recent N backup files (default: 0)
cli.help.restore: Restore from a backup file (supports ZIP / tar / tar.gz / tar.bz2 / tar.xz / tar.zst / 7z)

log.config.malformed: Config file %s is malformed, using defaults
log.config.reset: Config file %s is malformed, resetting to empty
log.data.create: Data file does not exist, creating: %s
err.sftp.auth: SFTP authentication failed: {host}, check username and password
err.sftp.not_connected: SFTP not connected, call connect() first
err.sftp.not_configured: SFTP not configured, run: sbackup sftp config --host <address> --user <username>
cmd.sftp.test_fail: SFTP connection test failed, check configuration
cli.help.save.sftp: Upload to SFTP server after backup (auto-detects SSH key or uses password)
cli.help.watch.sftp: Upload to SFTP server after each backup
cli.help.sftp: SFTP remote backup management (supports password and SSH key authentication)
cli.help.sftp.config: Configure SFTP connection (supports password and SSH key authentication)
cli.help.sftp.test: Test if SFTP connection is available
cli.help.sftp.remote_path: Remote target path (default: /, creates directory if not exists)
err.sftp.key_load: Cannot load private key {path}, check file format (RSA/Ed25519/ECDSA supported)
cli.help.sftp.key_file: SSH private key file path (RSA/Ed25519/ECDSA supported, takes priority over password)
cli.help.sftp.key_passphrase: Private key passphrase (for encrypted keys, can be entered interactively)
cli.prompt.sftp.key_file: SSH private key file path (leave empty for password auth):
cli.prompt.sftp.key_passphrase: Private key passphrase (press Enter to skip):
cmd.sftp.no_default_key: No default SSH key found, please configure password or specify key path
err.sftp.key_needs_passphrase: Private key {path} requires a passphrase, enter it or use --key-passphrase
err.sftp.wrong_passphrase: Wrong passphrase, please retry (press Enter to skip private key)
cli.help.webdav: WebDAV remote backup management (supports Jianguoyun, NextCloud, Synology, etc.)
cli.help.webdav.test: Test if WebDAV connection is available
cli.help.webdav.remote_path: Remote target path (default: /, creates directory if not exists)
cli.help.save.webdav: Upload to WebDAV server after backup
cli.help.watch.webdav: Upload to WebDAV server after each backup
err.webdav.auth: WebDAV authentication failed: {host}, check username and password
err.webdav.not_configured: WebDAV not configured, run: sbackup webdav config --url <address> --user <username>
err.webdav.mkdir: Failed to create WebDAV remote directory {path}: {error}
